title: Vehicle and parts salespersons and advisers
description: Receive and disburse money in establishments other than financial institutions. May use electronic scanners, cash registers, or related equipment. May process credit or debit card transactions and validate checks. Exchange coins, tokens, and chips for patrons' money. May issue payoffs and obtain customer's signature on receipt. May operate a booth in the slot machine area and furnish change persons with money bank at the start of the shift, or count and audit money in drawers. Sell spare and replacement parts and equipment in repair shop or parts store. (Based on 4 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Greet customers and ascertain what each customer wants or needs, and assist customers by providing information and resolving their complaints.

Monitor checkout stations to ensure they have adequate cash available and are staffed appropriately, and maintain clean and orderly checkout areas, completing other general cleaning duties as necessary.
Process merchandise returns and exchanges and exchange merchandise for customers, as well as issue receipts, refunds, credits, or change due to customers.
Compute sales prices, total purchases, and receive and process cash or credit payment, while also keeping accurate records of monetary exchanges and reconciling summaries of transactions.
Keep periodic balance sheets of amounts and numbers of transactions, and compile and maintain non-monetary reports and records.
Demonstrate use or operation of merchandise, describe merchandise and explain use, operation, and care of merchandise to customers, and answer questions regarding the store and its merchandise.
Locate and label parts, maintain inventory of stock, and inventory stock to requisition new stock.
Exchange money, credit, tickets, or casino chips and make change for customers, while also counting money and auditing money drawers, and checking identifications to verify age of players.

Measure parts using precision measuring instruments, determine replacement parts required according to inspections of old parts, and discuss use and features of various parts based on knowledge of machines or equipment.
Prepare merchandise for purchase or rental, bag or package purchases, and assist with duties in other areas, such as monitoring fitting rooms or bagging and carrying out customers' items.
Sell tickets and other items to customers, and sell spare and replacement parts and equipment in repair shop or parts store.
Help customers find the location of products and answer incoming phone calls.
Maintain knowledge of current sales and promotions, policies regarding payment and exchanges, and security practices.
Recommend, select, and help locate or obtain merchandise based on customer needs and desires, and provide a carry-out service at the completion of transactions.
Examine returned parts for defects, exchange defective parts, or refund money, as well as assist with trading stamps, food stamps, and coupons.
Supervise others and provide on-the-job training, and assist customers with special orders or call other stores to find desired items.
Sort, count, and wrap currency and coins, and clean casino areas when necessary, recording totals of transactions separately.
Weigh items sold by weight to determine prices and verify the adequacy of cash in drawers at the start of each shift.

Count money in cash drawers at the beginning of shifts to ensure that amounts are correct and that there is adequate change, and open and close cash registers, balancing cash drawers, and making deposits as required.
Keep records related to sales and estimate and quote trade-in allowances for merchandise.
Make deliveries and pick up parts, and operate booths in gaming areas while ensuring security according to rules and regulations.
Calculate total payments received during a time period, and reconcile with total sales, and analyze customer needs for potential rental of merchandise.
Provide check-cashing authorization or establish house credit accounts by accepting credit applications and verifying credit references.
Manage shipments by researching shipping methods or costs and tracking packages to ensure timely deliveries.
Operate cash registers, accepting payment by cash, check, credit cards, vouchers, or automatic debits for all transactions.
Assist with estimating quantities and costs for customer purchases, and provide a responsive service to help customers with their shopping requirements.
